provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Intelligence and Security Service Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Application
provisions.content:
3. Application
Unless the context otherwise requires, this Act applies to all members of staff of the Directorate.

provisions.chapter: PART II Directorate of Intelligence and Security
provisions.title: Establishment and composition of Directorate
provisions.content:
4. Establishment and composition of Directorate
(1) There is established, under the Office of the President, a Directorate to be known as the Directorate of Intelligence and Security.
(2) The Directorate shall comprise—
( a ) a Director General;
( b ) a Deputy Director General;
( c ) directors; and
( d ) such other officers and support staff as are referred to in section 10.

provisions.chapter: PART II Directorate of Intelligence and Security
provisions.title: Functions of Directorate

definitions.definition: information of such a sensitive nature and value that the unauthorised publication or disclosure thereof would lead to a security risk being posed to the State
the Central Intelligence Committee established by section 25(1)
the National Intelligence Community established by section 27(1)
the Intelligence and Security Council established by section 29
the Deputy Director General of the Directorate
a director of any of the divisions of the Directorate established under section 8(3)
the Director General of the Directorate
the Directorate of Intelligence and Security established by section 4
the spouse and children who are under the age of 21 years
information which relates to or may be relevant to any internal or external threat or potential threat to national security in any field

definitions.term: members of staff of the Directorate
definitions.definition: the Director General, the Deputy Director General, directors, officers and support staff

information which relates to any function concerning national security which by or under any law has been entrusted to any Government ministry, department or agency and which information may be required by that ministry, department or agency in the performance of any of its functions
the Intelligence and Security Parliamentary Committee established under section 38
an authorisation entitling a person to have access to information of a determined sensitivity or classification
a systematic procedure used to examine and appraise a person to determine his or her security competence, including the continued monitoring of that person
members of the Community who are not officers of the Directorate, but who are under the direction of the Director General, performing such duties as are provided for in this Act
